rulings.Case Title: RALPH WARNER et al VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-15-276410 WITH CASE CGC-10-275667
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 25, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Plaintiffs' motion to consolidate actions for all purposes including trial and to add consolidated cases to the December 17, 2015 trial setting conference is granted. No opposition filed. Cases nos. 275667 and 276410 are consolidated for all purposes including trial. The consolidated cases are added to the December 17, 2015 trial setting conference and the August 20, 2015 trial setting conference in case no. 275667 is continued to that date. Henceforth, all papers shall be filed in the lead case no. 275667. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JACK SOWERS et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.S Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 25, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. As to the causation issue, defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Walling's deposition testimony and declaration and Charles Ay's declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Sowers was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation) attributable to defendant. As to the duty issue, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant owed a duty to Mr. Sowers. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it did not demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive written discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their claim that defendant owed Mr. Sowers a duty. Defendant's objections to the declarations of Mr. Walling and Mr. Ay are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JACK SOWERS et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 25, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Timec Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Clifton's deposition testimony and declaration and Mr. Ay's declaration create a triable issue whether Jack Sowers was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation) attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to plaintiffs' supplemental response to special interrogatories are sustained and its objections to the declarations of Mr. Clifton and Mr. Ay and the deposition testimony of Mr. Clifton are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
